data.title: Russia, Ukraine agree to ceasefire for Orthodox Easter
data.description:
Russia and Ukraine have agreed to a two-day ceasefire for Orthodox Easter, after both Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for a truce during the holiday. 

The Kremlin announced that the ceasefire will be effective from April 11, 4:00 pm Moscow time, until the end of the day on April 12.

Russian troops have been instructed to cease combat activity on all fronts during this period and be prepared "to counter any provocation or other aggressive actions by the enemy," a statement from the Kremlin read.

"People need an Easter without threats and real progress toward peace, and Russia has a chance not to return to strikes even after Easter," Zelenskyy posted on X.

Meanwhile, the Ukrainian president called for the U.S. to reimpose sanctions on Russian oil as crude prices eased following the Iran ceasefire.

Zelenskyy said the U.S. rolled back the sanctions to stabilize the global oil market, adding that now, "there are effectively no grounds left to ease sanctions pressure."

"In my view, Russia played the Americans again – played the President of the United States," he later told Rai Radio 1 in an interview. "The Russians lied about the role their oil supplies played in keeping oil markets stable. If the U.S. reimposes sanctions, that would be fair."

The U.S. 30-day waiver for sales of sanctioned Russian oil and petroleum products currently ‌stranded ⁠at sea will expire on April 11.

data.title: OpenAI cites computing advantage over Anthropic to investors: report
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) told investors this week that its early efforts to increase computing resources give it a vital advantage over rival Anthropic (ANTHRO), Bloomberg News reported.

OpenAI said it has outpaced Anthropic by “rapidly and consistently” adding computing capacity to support wider adoption of its software, the report added, citing a note the company sent to some of its investors after Anthropic announced a more powerful AI model called Mythos.

Anthropic and OpenAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The ambitious infrastructure build-out, criticized by some as expensive, has enabled OpenAI to better keep pace with the growing demand for AI products, the memo noted.

"That gap matters because compute is now a product constraint,” said the ChatGPT maker in the note, according to the report.

In its note, OpenAI cited a report from analyst Ben Thompson, who authors the Stratechery blog, indicating that computing constraints may have impacted Anthropic’s decision to limit the release of Mythos to select partners, the report noted.

OpenAI said it had 1.9 gigawatts, or GW, of computing capacity available in 2025, triple from the year prior. Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI  expects that amount to increase to the "low-double-digit range" next year and reach around 30 GW by 2030. By comparison, OpenAI estimates Anthropic ended 2025 with 1.4 GW and will have between 7 GW and 8 GW of capacity next year, the report added.

“Even at the high end of that range, our ramp is materially ahead and widening,” said OpenAI in the note.

Anthropic has been increasing investments in physical infrastructure for its AI services, including a pledge to spend $50B to build data centers in the U.S. Earlier this week, Anthropic announced a deal with Google and Broadcom (AVGO) for multiple GW of next-generation TPU capacity starting in 2027. In all, reportedly, the deal would give Anthropic access to close to 5 GW in new computing capacity over the coming years.

The Claude AI chatbot maker — which is backed by Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google — in the past has generally taken a more conservative approach to spending than OpenAI, which intends to spend about $600B on data centers and chips by 2030. OpenAI recently raised $122B in funding to partly help pay for such commitments. However, the company has faced skepticism over the pace of its outlays.

On Thursday it was reported that OpenAI is pausing its Stargate AI infrastructure project in the U.K. as it reins in spending ahead of a potential public listing. Rival Anthropic is also preparing for a potential IPO.

In the memo, OpenAI characterized Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei as miscalculating the market’s appetite for more AI products. "In hindsight," the company said, "that caution looks less like discipline and more like underestimating how fast demand would arrive," the report noted.

data.title: Getlink March traffic: Passenger vehicles +8% Y/Y; truck freight -1%
Biggest stock movers Friday: SLP, TGLS, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were mixed Friday morning as the initial U.S.-Iran ceasefire relief rally faded, with doubts over its durability compounded by continued regional friction and minimal improvement in Strait of Hormuz tanker traffic, reintroducing a risk premium, pushing oil back toward the $100 mark, and keeping equity gains in check.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Simulations Plus (SLP) +14% - Shares surged after a Q2 beat, with non-GAAP EPS of $0.35 topping estimates and revenue rising 8.3% Y/Y to $24.3M, also ahead of expectations. While FY2026 revenue guidance of $79M–$82M remains broadly in line with consensus and other operating metrics were unchanged, the company lowered its adjusted EPS outlook to $0.75–$0.85 (from $1.03–$1.10) due to a higher expected tax rate. Investors appeared encouraged by solid execution and margin targets of 26%–30% EBITDA alongside a strong software revenue mix outlook.
  • Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) +2% – Shares rose after reporting strong March 2026 revenue of NT$415.19B, up 45.2% Y/Y and 30.7% sequentially, driven by robust demand for AI-related chips; Q1 revenue totaled NT$1.13T, slightly ahead of consensus and up 35.1% Y/Y, reinforcing momentum in advanced-node demand. Investors now focused on the company’s full Q1 earnings report scheduled for April 16.

Biggest stock losers

  • Tecnoglass (TGLS) -6% - Shares fell after the company updated its FY2026 outlook to reflect the impact of a newly implemented 10% U.S. tariff on aluminum window imports, now guiding for adjusted EBITDA of $225M–$245M, implying a ~$50M hit vs. the prior midpoint. While management outlined mitigation steps, including pricing actions starting with May orders, logistics optimization, increased automation, and headcount rationalization, it expects only a partial offset in 2026, with full tariff neutralization targeted by 2027.

data.title: Lindt completes buyback early, launches new program in May
data.description:
• Swiss chocolate maker Lindt (LDSVF) completed its buyback program, which started on August 2, 2024, ahead of schedule on April 9, 2026. The company said the new buyback program will start in May 2026. 
  • As announced on March 10, 2026, Chocoladefabriken Lindt & Sprüngli AG decided to launch a new buyback program for registered shares and participation certificates of up to CHF 1 billion, which will run for up to three years.
  • Due to the completion of the existing buyback program ahead of schedule, the new buyback program will already start in May 2026. Separate trading lines will be opened on SIX Swiss Exchange for the new buyback program of registered shares and participation certificates.

data.title: European markets subdued as oil surge clouds outlook
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.15% to 10,618

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.18% to 23,834. Germany's inflation touches highest level since January 2024

France (CAC:IND) +0.08% to 8,252.

In other parts of Europe, the Czech unemployment rate fell to 5% in March.

Switzerland’s consumer sentiment index fell sharply to -43 in March.

Norway’s annual inflation rate accelerated to 3.6% in March.

Industrial production in Slovakia dropped by 2.9% Y/Y in February.

Industrial production in Sweden rose by 7.0% Y/Y in February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.29% higher to 614.4, supported by optimism over a U.S.-Iran ceasefire that could potentially lead to a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. However, crude oil prices surged toward $100 mark as geopolitical tensions continued to dominate market direction. The prospects of improved shipping conditions have bolstered risk sentiment across global markets. 

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up less than 1 basis point to 4.30%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points to 4.80%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 3.02%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: iQIYI eyes theme park in Wuhan, slated for 2027
data.description:
• China’s streaming platform iQIYI (IQ) plans to develop its first theme park in Central China, marking its first project integrated into an urban renewal initiative and physical entertainment experiences.
  • The company announced plans to build iQIYI LAND in Wuhan, with the project slated to open in 2027.
  • The park will be located within the Hanyangzao Cultural and Creative Industry Park and is positioned to create a new cultural tourism landmark.
  • The park will feature immersive theaters, live performances, film and TV scene recreations, character interactions, and social gaming experiences, all built around iQIYI’s portfolio of Chinese dramas, animation, and variety content.
  • The Wuhan project builds on the early success of iQIYI LAND Yangzhou, which opened in February 2026 as the company’s first immersive theme park and has received strong user ratings on local platforms.

data.title: Intel, Google team up for AI and cloud infrastructure
data.description:
Intel (INTC) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google entered a multiyear collaboration to advance the next generation of AI and cloud infrastructure with Xeon CPUs and custom infrastructure processing units, or IPUs.

Under the collaboration, the two companies will align across multiple generations of Intel's Xeon processors to improve performance, energy efficiency, and total cost of ownership across Google’s global infrastructure.

The companies said they are expanding their co-development of custom application-specific integrated circuit, or ASIC-based IPUs. These programmable accelerators offload networking, storage, and security functions from host CPUs, thereby increasing efficiency and enabling more predictable performance across hyperscale AI environments, according to the companies.

"CPUs and IPUs are central to delivering the performance, efficiency and flexibility modern AI workloads demand," said Intel's CEO Lip-Bu Tan.

data.title: Agree Realty raises monthly dividend by 1.9% to $0.267 a share
data.description:
• Agree Realty (ADC) declares $0.267/share monthly dividend, 1.9% increase from prior dividend of $0.262.
  • Forward yield 4.11%
  • Payable May 14; for shareholders of record April 30; ex-div April 30.
  • The company raised its monthly dividend by 1.9% after paying a monthly dividend of $0.262 in each of the previous 6 months.
  • See ADC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: White House warns staff against placing bets on Polymarket, Kalshi
data.description:
The White House warned its staff against using insider information to place bets on prediction markets like Polymarket (POLYMARKET) and Kalshi (KALSHI), according to multiple media reports, citing officials.

"Recent press reports have raised concerns about government officials using nonpublic government information to place wagers on online prediction markets," the White House Management Office wrote in a staff-wide email last month. 

"All White House employees are reminded that the misuse of nonpublic information by government employees for financial benefit is a very serious offense and will not be tolerated," it added, noting that buying or selling prediction market contracts using classified information was a criminal offense.

The email was sent a day after President Donald Trump announced on social media a five-day pause on strikes against Iran.

Around 15 minutes before his announcement, a flurry of activity kicked off in the futures markets. Over $760M worth of oil futures contracts changed hands in under two minutes, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing Dow Jones Market Data.

Three accounts on Polymarket earned over $600,000 by betting on the timing of this week's ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

"President Trump has been crystal clear: while he seeks a strong and profitable stock market for everyone, members of Congress and other government officials should be prohibited from using nonpublic information for financial benefit," White House spokesman Davis Ingle said.

"Any implication that administration officials are engaged in such activity without evidence is baseless and irresponsible reporting," he added.

There is no evidence that anyone within the Trump administration is using inside information to place well-timed bets on prediction markets.
